It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|12=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|12=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|12=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|12=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|12=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|12=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|12=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|12=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|12=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|12=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|12=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|12=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|12=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|12=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|12=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|12=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|12=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|12=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|12=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|12=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|12=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|12=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|12=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|12=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|12=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|12=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|12=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|12=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|12=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|12=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft|12=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|12=draft|12=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft|12=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|12=draft|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|12=draft|12=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|12=draft|12=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft|12=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|12=draft|12=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|12=draft|12=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|12=draft|12=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|12=draft|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|12=draft|12=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|12=draft|12=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|12=draft|12=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Sagae Hachimangu|Sagae Hachimangu]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] }} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Sagae, Yamagata]] [[Category:Ōe clan|Shrine Sagae Hachimangu]] [[Category:Sagae clan|Shrine Sagae Hachimangu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sagae, Yamagata]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshiie]] [[Category:Ōe no Chikahiro]] [[Category:Yamagata Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:寒河江八幡宮 up to rev 102089141
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=寒河江八幡宮 | 画像=[[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[山形県]][[寒河江市]]八幡町5-70 | 位置={{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=寒河江八幡宮}} | 祭神=[[誉田別命]]
[[大山祇神]] | 創建=[[寛治]]7年（[[1093年]]） | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] | 例祭=[[9月15日]] | 地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|label=寒河江
八幡宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E}}}} }} '''寒河江八幡宮'''（さがえはちまんぐう）は、[[山形県]][[寒河江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。[[寒河江荘]]の[[鎮守神|総鎮守]]で、かつては'''川西鎮守'''と称した。 [[誉田別命]]（[[応神天皇]]、[[八幡神]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[大山祇神]]を配祀する。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[源頼義]]・[[源義家]]親子が[[前九年の役]]の際八幡神に祈って戦勝したので、[[寛治]]7年（[[1093年]]）に京都男山八幡宮を勧請し八幡原（現・[[寒河江市]]若葉町・元町）に創建したと伝える旧八幡社は現在失われて八幡原第三公園となっているが、戦国時代の[[寒河江城]]を描いた『寒河江城古絵図』〔天文3年（[[1534年]]）制作（天正4年（[[1576年]]）銘）〕には旧社が八幡原に描かれている。。[[鎌倉時代]]の[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）、寒河江に入った[[大江親広]]が改めて[[鎌倉]]にある[[鶴岡八幡宮]]から分霊を勧請し、八幡原の八幡社と合祀して現在地に社殿を創建して[[大江氏]]（[[寒河江氏]]）の[[産土神]]とした。[[谷地八幡宮]]・[[溝延八幡神社]]とともに「寒河江荘三八幡」と称され、武家の崇敬を受けた。現在の本殿は[[貞享]]3年（[[1686年]]）に拝殿は[[安永]]4年（[[1775年]]）に建築された。[[1940年]]（昭和15年）に県社に昇格した。 元の社名は「八幡神社」であったが、神社本庁の別表神社に加列される際に現在の寒河江八幡宮に改称した。 == 文化財 == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|古式流鏑馬]] === 県指定文化財　工芸品 === *短刀　銘出羽寒河江月山 *:室町時代の作。 === 県指定無形民俗文化財 === *寒河江八幡宮[[流鏑馬]] *:前日祭および例大祭で行われる、古式流鏑馬と作試し流鏑馬。古式流鏑馬は騎乗した騎手が150メートルの馬場において、疾走する馬上から3か所の的を射て作柄を占う。3頭の馬において行う。作試し流鏑馬はあらかじめ3頭の馬に早稲・中稲・晩稲と決めておき着順で翌年の作柄を占う。全国でも寒河江八幡宮のみに現存する。 == 年中行事 == *1月1日：歳旦祭（初詣）古神札お焚き上げ *2月11日：紀元節祭・建国記念日奉祝式典 *2月（旧暦初午）：稲荷神社（境内末社）初午祭 *4月28日：新山神社祭（境内末社）市内南町新山講 *4月29日：講社大祭（春祭り） *5月16日：厳島神社（末社）例祭 *6月30日：茅輪神事・夏越大祓 *7月1日：水神祭 *9月9日：流鏑馬参籠始祭（例大祭迄1週間のオコモリ禊潔斎） *9月14日：前日祭（南部地区渡御・古式流鏑馬奉納） *9月15日：例大祭（本町地区渡御・作試し流鏑馬奉納）東北一の大神輿渡御・神輿の祭典 *9月16日：終了報告祭（流鏑馬参籠オトキ） *11月23日：新嘗祭（氏子青年神輿會による御神田献米） *12月13日：煤払い *12月31日：晦（つごもり・年越）大祓・除夜祭・鎮火祭 == ギャラリー == ファイル:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|鳥居 ファイル:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|参道 ファイル:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|裏参道 ファイル:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|春の厳島神社（末社） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ==外部リンク== {{Wikinews|2015年のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015年9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org 寒河江八幡宮]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかえはちまんくう}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:寒河江市の歴史]] [[Category:大江氏|社さかえはちまんくう]] [[Category:寒河江氏|社さかえはちまんくう]] [[Category:寒河江市の建築物]] [[Category:源頼義]] [[Category:源義家]] [[Category:大江親広]] [[Category:山形県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Sagae Hachimangu|Sagae Hachimangu]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}}
