Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sagae Hachimangu}} {{translated page|ja|寒河江八幡宮|version=102089141|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] }} [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] Hachimangu]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Sagae Hachimangu|Sagae Hachimangu]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|寒河江八幡宮|version=102089141|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] }} [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] [[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[ja:%E5%AF%92%E6%B2%B3%E6%B1%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[ja:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year |lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year |lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year |lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|寒河江八幡宮|version=102089141|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] }} [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] [[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[ja:%E5%AF%92%E6%B2%B3%E6%B1%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[ja:Sagae Hachimangu]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Sagae Hachimangu|Sagae Hachimangu]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 20:44, 2 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg }} Sagae Hachimangu is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji era|qq=}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year 1093|qq=}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Jinja Honchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae-shō|qq=}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkunen War|qq=}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year|lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū era|qq=}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe clan|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae clan|qq=}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachi Hachimangū|qq=}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|qq=}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year|lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|qq=}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year|lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An’ei 4|qq=}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|寒河江八幡宮|version=102089141|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] }} [[every:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sagae Hachimangu]] [[sn:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:draft:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[en:Sagae Hachimangu]] [[ja:%E5%AF%92%E6%B2%B3%E6%B1%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[ja:Sagae Hachimangu]] Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji era|qq=}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year 1093|qq=}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae-shō|qq=}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkunen War|qq=}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year|lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū era|qq=}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe clan|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae clan|qq=}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachi Hachimangū|qq=}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|qq=}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year|lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|qq=}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year|lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An’ei 4|qq=}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji era|qq=}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year 1093|qq=}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae-shō|qq=}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkunen War|qq=}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year|lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū era|qq=}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe clan|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae clan|qq=}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachi Hachimangū|qq=}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|qq=}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year|lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|qq=}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year|lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An’ei 4|qq=}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]]. {{cite web |last=Scheid |first=Bernhard |title=Hachiman Shreine |url=http://www.univie.ac.at/rel_jap/bauten/bekannteschreine.htm |access-date=17 August 2010 |publisher=University of Vienna |language=de}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sagae Hachimangū | image = [[File:Sagae Hachimangu Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall (Haiden) | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamagata | coordinates = {{Coord|38|22|41.8|N|140|16|14.7|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sagae Hachimangū}} | location = {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}} Hachimanchō 5-70 | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, Hachiman deity)
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji era|qq=}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year 1093|qq=}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae-shō|qq=}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|deity|lt=deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkunen War|qq=}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year|lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū era|qq=}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe clan|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae clan|qq=}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachi Hachimangū|qq=}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|qq=}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year|lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|qq=}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year|lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An’ei 4|qq=}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|Kanji era|ja|寛治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanji era|qq=}} 7 ({{ill|Year 1093|ja|1093rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Year 1093|qq=}}) | type = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | festival = {{ill|September 15|ja|9月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15|qq=}} }} '''Sagae Hachimangū''' (寒河江八幡宮, Sagae Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sagae, Yamagata|ja|寒河江市|lt=Sagae City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae, Yamagata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. Under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system, it was a [[Prefectural Shrine]] (Kensha), and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo Shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It served as the general guardian deity (sōchinju) of the {{ill|Sagae-shō|ja|寒河江荘|lt=Sagae-shō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae-shō|qq=}}, and was once called “Kawanishi Chinju” (川西鎮守). It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|lt=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the Hachiman deity) as its main {{ill|Saijin|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}} along with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|lt=Ōyama-tsumi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} as a subordinate deity. == History == According to shrine tradition, during the {{ill|Zenkunen War|ja|前九年の役|lt=Before the Nine Years' War|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenkunen War|qq=}} (also known as the Early Nine Years' War), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|ja|源頼義|lt=Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriie|qq=}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed to the Hachiman deity for victory. After their success, in the Kanji 7 year (1093), they invited the deity from the {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman-gū in Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} and founded the shrine at Hachimanbara (present-day Wakaba-chō and Motomachi in Sagae City).The old Hachiman Shrine no longer exists and has been replaced by Hachimanbara Daisan Park. However, the “Sagae-jō Ko-ezu” (Old Picture map of Sagae Castle) from the Tenshō era (1534 CE) includes the old shrine at Hachimanbara. In the {{ill|Kenkyū era|ja|建久2nd year|lt=Kenkyū 2 (1191)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū era|qq=}} of the Kamakura period, when {{ill|Ōe no Chikahiro|ja|大江親広|lt=Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe no Chikahiro|qq=}} came to Sagae, he once again invited a divided spirit from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura, merging it with the Hachiman deity already at Hachimanbara, and established a shrine building at its current site. It was revered as the tutelary deity (ubusunagami) of the {{ill|Ōe clan|ja|大江氏|lt=Ōe-shi (Ōe clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōe clan|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Sagae clan|ja|寒河江氏|lt=Sagae-shi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae clan|qq=}}). Together with {{ill|Yachi Hachimangū|ja|谷地八幡宮|lt=Yachi Hachimangū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yachi Hachimangū|qq=}} and {{ill|Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|ja|溝延八幡神社|lt=Mizone no Hachiman Jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonoeb Hachiman Jinja|qq=}}, it was called one of the “Three Hachiman Shrines of Sagae-shō” and held in high esteem by the warrior families. The current main hall (honden) was built in {{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3rd year|lt=Jōkyō 3 (1686)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|qq=}} and the worship hall (haiden) in {{ill|An’ei 4|ja|安永4th year|lt=An’ei 4 (1775)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An’ei 4|qq=}}. In 1940 (Shōwa 15), it was elevated to prefectural shrine status. Originally named “Hachiman Jinja,” the shrine adopted its present name “Sagae Hachimangū” when it was added to the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Jinja Honchō. == Cultural Properties == [[File:SagaeHachimangu Yabusame2013.jpg|220px|thumb|Ancient-style yabusame]] === Prefectural Cultural Property (Craft) === * Tantō (short sword) inscribed as “Dewa Sagae Gassan” *: Made during the Muromachi period. === Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yabusame at Sagae Hachimangū *: Held during the eve of the festival and the main festival, this includes ancient-style yabusame and “sakudameshi yabusame.” In ancient-style yabusame, mounted archers ride at full speed along a 150-meter course and shoot three targets. Three horses are used, and the performance divines harvest prospects. In “sakudameshi yabusame,” three horses are designated in advance as early-ripening, mid-ripening, and late-ripening rice; their finishing order predicts next year’s harvest. Sagae Hachimangū’s yabusame tradition is unique in Japan. == annual Events == * January 1: Saitan-sai (New Year’s festival), Burning of old amulets * February 11: Kigensetsu-sai & National Foundation Day Commemorative ceremony * February (Lunisolar’s first Horse Day): Inari Jinja (subsidiary shrine) Hatsuumafestival * April 28: Shinzan Jinja festival (subsidiary shrine), organized by local Minami-machi Shinzan Kō * April 29: Kōsha Taisai (Spring festival) * May 16: Itsukushima Jinja (subsidiary shrine) festival * June 30: Chinowa-shinji, Nagoshi no Ōharae (Summer Purification) * July 1: Water deity festival * September 9: Yabusame Sango Hajime-sai (One-week purity retreat/misogi for Yabusame until the main festival) * September 14: Eve of the Grand festival (Nambu district procession, ancient-style yabusame dedication) * September 15: annual Grand festival (Honmachi district procession, sakudameshi yabusame dedication), Tōhoku’s largest mikoshi procession and festival * September 16: Closing ceremony (End of Yabusame Sango purification) * November 23: Niiname-sai (new harvest festival), offering of sacred rice by the parish youth mikoshi association * December 13: Soot sweeping * December 31: Tsugomori (year-end) Ōharae (Great Purification), Joyasai (Year-end festival), Chinka-sai (Fire-quelling ceremony) == Gallery == File:SagaeHachimangu Sandou2.JPG|Torii gate File:SagaeHachimangu sandou1.JPG|Approach (Sandō) File:SagaeHachimangu_Urasandou.JPG|Rear approach (Ura-sandō) File:SagaeItukusima Sakura.jpg|Itsukushima Shrine (subsidiary shrine) in spring == Notes == == External Links == {{Wikinews|2015th year のシルバーウィークに行われた全国各地の「流鏑馬」|date=2015th year 9月27日}} *[http://www.sagae-hachimangu.org/ Sagae Hachimangū Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |work=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} In Japanese religion, '''''Yahata''''' (八幡神, ancient [[Shinto]] pronunciation) formerly in Shinto and later commonly known as '''Hachiman''' (八幡神, [[Buddhism in Japan|Japanese Buddhist]] pronunciation) is the [[Shinbutsu-shūgō|syncretic divinity]] of archery and war,{{cite book |last1=Kanda |first1=Christine Guth |title=Shinzō: Hachiman imagery and Its Development |series=Harvard East Asian Monographs |date=1985 |volume=119 |doi=10.2307/j.ctt1tg5jkx |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1tg5jkx |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center}}{{cite journal |last1=Law |first1=Jane Marie |title=Violence, Ritual Reenactment, and Ideology: The "Hōjō-e" (Rite for Release of Sentient Beings) of the USA Hachiman Shrine in Japan |journal=History of Religions |date=1994 |volume=33 |issue=4 |pages=325–357 |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1062714 |issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite web |title=Hachiman & Hachimangū Shrines |url=http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/tsurugaoka-hachiman.shtml |quote=It was only later, sometime in the 9th century, that the deity became associated with Emperor Ōjin, and later still that Hachiman became worshipped as the god of archery and war, ultimately becoming the tutelary deity of the Minamoto clan and its famed warrior Minamoto Yoritomo 源頼朝 (1147–99), founder of the Kamakura shogunate}} incorporating elements from both [[Shinto]] and [[Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism]].
